Making an early run for a gold medal in the category of Olympics superfan is Hollywood superstarNicole Kidman, who is heading to France to see our beloved Matildas battle Germany in one of the Games’ first events.

Kidman will jet into the French port city of Marseille for the women’s soccer match on July 25 in the Stade de Marseille, just ahead of the Olympics’ opening ceremony in Paris on July 26, we think as a guest of official timekeeper Omega. No doubt the Matildas,who lost their final friendly match 1-2 to defending gold medallists Canada on the weekend, will hope Kidman’s gold medal-winning track record (an Oscar in 2003 for playingVirginia WoolfinThe Hours) will inspire them.

Jessica Mauboy (left) and Nicole Kidman are ready for some Aussie gold.Credit:Joe Benke

Also attending the Matildas match is singerJessica Mauboy,who has recordedHigher, the official song of the Australian Olympic team. The track,written byShannon Busch, known professionally asWilsnand producerStephen Mowat, received input from chef de missionAnna Mearesand four-time Olympian and AOC Athletes Commission chairCate Campbell, who rates the track as a “killer”.

Mauboy will also be in the stands for some Hockeyroos games. Turns out she was a keen hockey player back in Darwin before her life turned a corner when she was 16 thanks toAustralian Idol. Who knew? The Australian Olympic Committee evidently did.

Communications Minister Michelle Rowland and husband Michael.Credit:Getty Images

Communications MinisterMichelle Rowlandand husbandMichael Chaayawereenjoying themselves on Parliament House’s grand marble staircase at the recent Midwinter Ballas they roped in a passing journo to snap a tricky photo of them while avoiding getting photobombed by nearby Woodside Energy bossMeg O’Neill. Happy days.

But it’s a time of considerable personnel churn in the minister’s office, with frankly little policy action in the big communications portfolio reform plate, save for last week’s passage of anti-siphoning laws, which were a major win for the AFL, NRL and Foxtel.

In news just in, Rowland has hired the ABC’sMichael Reidas a new press secretary. He’s a veteran documentary maker and television journalist, who won a Walkley for his coverage of the 2020 bushfires.

Can someone gently alert Opposition LeaderPeter Dutton, who gets pretty steamed up about journalists heading off to work for the Albanese government, despite it being a fairly common occurrence?

And speaking of churn, Rowland’s current spinner,Adam Boidin, is joining Optus in a non-media role.Arley Black, who worked for Education MinisterJason Clarein opposition and headed up Rowland’s comms since the 2022 election, left politics to work for Auspost last year.Pnina Hagege, who headed digital communications when Labor came into government, now works for “leading multi-partisan public affairs advisory” GRACosway, as it styles itself.

Meanwhile,Maggie Lloyd, a formerKevin Rudd-era junior staffer was deputy chief of staff to Rowland, but also left recently to become chief of staff to infrastructure ministerCatherine King.

Why so? We did ask the office, but didn’t hear back. We hear Rowland is a good boss, but the office is a bit disorganised. Maybe the lack of a junior minister in the sprawling communications portfolio (for the first time in a long time) is having an impact.

Luckily for Rowland, there are always plenty of underpaid journalists with in-depth knowledge of the whole media thing ready to switch to the dark side for a taste of government.

Assistant Foreign Minister Tim Watts.Credit:ABC

We sometimes forget that our politicians, before entering parliament and getting their individuality crushed by the party machine, indulged in a few offbeat hobbies.

Labor’s Assistant Foreign Minister,Tim Watts, a keen gamer, once wrote of hoping to induct his children into playingMario Karton Nintendo Switch, but he had “already reached my console limit at home”.

Watts isn’t particularly unique in his love of reading, but his book-related Tumblr back in the 2010s,Blogging the Bookshelf, reveals a voracious reader with a taste in literature miles away from turgid Churchill biographies, wonkish geopolitical tomes and dad histories that our elected representatives seem to mostly consume.

Watts’ blog assesses each book with either a “buy,” “borrow,” or “toss”.

The member for Gellibrand in Melbourne stopped blogging in 2012, the year before he was elected, but is still an avid reader. He told CBD: “At the moment I’m reading Kim Ghattas’Black Wave.”It’s an examination of Middle East geopolitics and sounds way too much like homework to us, buddy.

Watts the blogger was particularly blown away by Soviet writerVasily Grossman’sWWII historical epicLife and Fate.More intriguing is his love for Japanese writerYukio Mishima,who ended up embracing the far right and died by seppuku, a form of Japanese ritualistic suicide, after trying to stage an unsuccessful coup.

And as a Labor man, giving only a “borrow” to ex-foreign minister and NSW premierBob Carr’sMy Life in Readingmust sting.

As far as we can tell, the only book to make it onto Watts’ “toss” pile was legendary literary criticHarold Bloom’s–The Western Canon: The Books and School of the Ages.The Watts’ verdict? “The bulk is reactionary pomposity.”

Spotted:SuccessionstarSarah Snook, fresh from her UK triumph inA Picture of Dorian Gray, at Arts Centre Melbourne’s Playhouse on the opening night of Melbourne Theatre Company’sA Streetcar Named Desire. She was in the queue for the ladies.

title: Nicole Kidman early shot at gold medal as Olympics superfan
author: Stephen Brook, Kishor Napier-Raman
description: CBD: Nicole Kidman early shot at gold medal as Olympics superfan
